name: BEA-2019 W&I+LOCNESS
availability: W&I+LOCNESS v2.1 listed as downloadable for non-commercial use; other corpora have mixed direct download and request flows.
data_shape: M2 GEC format; W&I levels A, B, C plus LOCNESS native N; 3700 texts, 43169 sentences, 801361 tokens across train/dev/test.
slopless_use: Use as source-only metadata plus future grammar-fixture mining. Do not convert broad grammar corrections into default Slopless rules.

name: CLC FCE Dataset
availability: Direct download after agreeing to Cambridge non-commercial license.
data_shape: 1244 FCE exam scripts with original text, marks, error annotation, and demographic metadata.
slopless_use: Useful for grammar-only source notes, word-choice fixtures, and no-hit controls for learner English.

name: NUCLE
data_shape: SGML learner essays with stand-off character-offset corrections; about 1400 essays and over one million words.
slopless_use: Source-only grammar taxonomy; possible deterministic candidates only for redundancy and transition misuse.

name: JFLEG
availability: Public GitHub repository under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
data_shape: 1511 source sentences; dev and test splits; 4 human fluency references per sentence.
slopless_use: High-value for revision-pair corpus ideas; risky for deterministic grammar rules.

name: cLang-8 / Lang-8
availability: cLang-8 targets public; source sentences require separate Lang-8 form and download.
data_shape: TSV source-target pairs after local alignment; English has 2372119 examples.
slopless_use: Large-scale grammar correction source only; use for corpus sampling, not rule phrases.

name: EFCAMDAT
availability: Requires academic affiliation, Google Drive, and approval.
data_shape: Second release has 1180310 texts from 174743 learners; XML, cleaned XLSX, error-coded subcorpus, prompts.
slopless_use: Excellent for learner-level corpus design and no-hit controls; access-gated.

name: Write & Improve Corpus 2024
availability: Licensed download; no redistribution; excerpts under 100 words only under license terms.
data_shape: 5050 user-prompt sets from 766 L2 learners; 23000 essays including revisions; first and final versions annotated with grammatical errors; final versions have CEFR labels.
slopless_use: Best fit for future private revision-pair mining if license permits.

name: ELLIPSE Corpus
availability: GitHub files available; test/raw zips are password-protected with passwords in README.
data_shape: About 6500 final ELL writing samples with holistic and analytic scores; larger raw rater file has about 9000 essays.
slopless_use: Score categories map to corpus stratification and false-positive tests, not deterministic rules.

name: PERSUADE 2.0
availability: GitHub metadata; data files hosted on Google Drive; test zip is password-protected.
data_shape: Over 25000 argumentative essays from grades 6-12; 15 prompts; independent and source-based tasks.
slopless_use: Use for argument-structure corpus ideas, weak evidence fixtures, and conclusion risks.

name: DREsS
availability: Code public; WikiSmall/WikiLarge links public; Newsela requires Newsela access.
data_shape: Sentence simplification model resources; source-target simplification pairs; multi-reference WikiLarge dev/test via TurkCorpus.
slopless_use: Use for sentence-length and simplification corpus ideas, not direct slop labels.

name: ArgRewrite V.2
availability: Public GitHub repository with 4.6 MB zip plus unpacked essays, annotations, and metadata.
data_shape: 258 essays, 172 Excel annotation files, 3238 sentential revisions, 2596 subsentential revisions.
slopless_use: Strong source for revision fixture ideas and precision/clarity category tests.

name: USC First-Year English Corpus
availability: ZIP files listed by semester on WAC Clearinghouse; non-commercial academic research.
data_shape: 17246 papers; 8575 matched first/final draft pairs; about 22 million words; plain ASCII text.
slopless_use: Best open draft-pair source for revision trajectory corpus ideas.

name: CROW
availability: Access request required for full corpus/repository; offline representative subset requires training.
data_shape: Over 18000 undergraduate first-year writing texts plus 380 instructional materials.
slopless_use: Use for assignment/draft-aware corpus design and multilingual writer false positives.

name: LEAF
availability: Public GitHub JSONL; 31 MB full file not mirrored.
data_shape: Repository version has leaf.jsonl with essay, human feedback, AI-augmented feedback, and split fields.
slopless_use: Useful for feedback-language risks and AI-feedback slop no-hits; not clean human revision pairs.

name: ERRANT
data_shape: No corpus; extracts, classifies, and evaluates edits in M2 format.
slopless_use: Use as normalization vocabulary for future correction-pair mining.

name: ASSET
data_shape: 2000 validation and 359 test originals, each with 10 simplifications.
slopless_use: Use for simplification examples and sentence-splitting no-hit controls.

name: MinWikiSplit
availability: Preprocessed Hugging Face dataset is public; original scale is large, so only metadata captured.
data_shape: 193226 examples in preprocessed HF version; original paper reports 203K aligned complex-source and simplified-target pairs.
slopless_use: Use for splitting and readability corpus ideas; not direct writing-quality labels.
